error.signature: GraphQL query takes 30s+ with nested resolvers due to N+1 database queries

verdict.summary: GraphQL resolvers execute per-field per-item. A query returning 100 users with posts generates 101 SQL queries. DataLoader batching fixes this but must be per-request scoped.

dead_ends.action: Add database indexes to speed up individual resolver queries
dead_ends.why_fails: Indexes help per-query speed but don't reduce the 100+ query count. The bottleneck is round-trip overhead.

dead_ends.action: Create a single global DataLoader instance
dead_ends.why_fails: DataLoader cache must be per-request. A global instance leaks data between users and grows unbounded.

workarounds.action: Create DataLoader instances per-request in context
workarounds.how: context = { userLoader: new DataLoader(ids => User.findByIds(ids)) }  // new per request

workarounds.action: Use query complexity analysis to reject expensive queries
